title: L7: Absolute Cell Referencing
description: Use the $ symbol to lock cell references when copying formulas, and access data stored in other worksheets for BTEC DIT Component 2.

steps.title: Do Now: Cell References
steps.content:
Do now

These questions check the formula and referencing skills you need before learning about absolute cell referencing.
steps.js: createMCQSet('l7-dn',[{q:'A formula in D3 is =B3*C3. It is copied one row down to D4. What does D4 now contain?',options:['=B3*C3','=B4*C4','=B4*C3','=B3*C4'],answer:1,right:'Correct. Both references are relative, so both row numbers increase by 1 when copied down.'},{q:'Which formula adds all values in the range B2 to B8?',options:['=COUNT(B2:B8)','=TOTAL(B2:B8)','=SUM(B2:B8)','=ADD(B2:B8)'],answer:2,right:'Correct. SUM adds every value in the range. COUNT counts how many there are, not the total.'},{q:'Which formula counts how many cells in B2:B10 contain the word Shop?',options:['=COUNTIF(B2:B10,"Shop")','=SUMIF(B2:B10,"Shop",B2:B10)','=COUNT(B2:B10,"Shop")','=IF(B2:B10,"Shop",1)'],answer:0,right:'Correct. COUNTIF counts cells matching a condition. SUMIF would add values, not count them.'},{q:'In a spreadsheet cell reference like C5, what do the letter and number represent?',options:['The row letter and the column number','The sheet name and the position in that sheet','The formula identifier and result','The column letter and the row number'],answer:3,right:'Correct. Letters identify columns (A, B, C...) and numbers identify rows (1, 2, 3...).'},{q:'What does the formula bar show when you click a cell that contains a formula?',options:['The formatted display value only','The formula (e.g. =A2*B2), not the calculated result','The cell address only','The name of the worksheet'],answer:1,right:'Correct. The formula bar always shows the raw formula, not the number you see displayed in the cell.'}]);

steps.title: Relative Referencing
steps.content:
Relative cell referencing

The cell references you have used so far are called relative references. When you copy a formula, relative references update automatically to reflect the new row or column position.

CellFormulaWhat happened
C2=A2*B2Original formula
C3=A3*B3Copied down — row numbers increased by 1
C4=A4*B4Copied down again — row numbers increased again

This is useful when every row should use the values from its own row. But there is a problem: what if every row needs to reference the same fixed cell?

Imagine a tax rate is stored in cell B1. You write =B3*B1 in C3. When copied to C4 it becomes =B4*B2 — it no longer references the tax rate in B1. The formula is broken.

CellIntended formulaWhat copying actually produces
C3=B3*B1 ✓Correct — B1 is the tax rate
C4=B4*B1 (intended)=B4*B2 (wrong) — relative copy moved B1 to B2
C5=B5*B1 (intended)=B5*B3 (wrong) — now pointing to a price, not the tax rate

The solution is to use an absolute cell reference so that B1 never changes when copied.
steps.js: createMCQSet('l7-rel',[{q:'A formula =A2+B2 is copied one row down. What is the new formula?',options:['=A3+B3','=A2+B3','=A3+B2','=A2+B2'],answer:0,right:'Correct. Both references are relative so both row numbers increase by 1.'},{q:'A formula =B3*C3 is copied three rows down. What does the copied cell contain?',options:['=B3*C3','=B6*C3','=B6*C6','=B3*C6'],answer:2,right:'Correct. Each relative reference shifts down by 3 rows, so row 3 becomes row 6.'},{q:'The tax rate is in D1. A formula =B3*D1 is copied from row 3 to row 4. What is the problem?',options:['D1 is automatically locked so no problem occurs','The formula is deleted when copied','The copy produces =B4*D2, so D1 (the tax rate) is no longer referenced','The formula is copied as =B3*D1 in both cells'],answer:2,right:'Correct. The relative copy moves D1 to D2, which is not the tax rate cell.'},{q:'When is relative referencing useful?',options:['When every row must always reference one single fixed cell','When copying would cause an error','When you want to reference a different worksheet only','When each row should calculate using its own values in the same columns'],answer:3,right:'Correct. Relative referencing is ideal when each row needs its own calculation using corresponding values.'}]);

steps.title: Absolute Cell Referencing
steps.content:
Absolute cell referencing

An absolute reference uses a dollar sign $ to lock the column, the row, or both. A locked part never changes when the formula is copied.

ReferenceMeaningWhen copied in any direction
$A$4Column A fixed, row 4 fixedAlways refers to cell A4
$A4Column A fixed, row relativeColumn stays A; row number updates
A$4Column relative, row 4 fixedColumn letter updates; row stays 4
A4Both relativeBoth column and row update (normal)

The most common form for a fixed constant (such as a tax rate, a price per unit, or PI) is $A$4 — locking both the column and the row.

Fixing the tax rate problem with $B$1:

CellFormulaResult
C3=B3*$B$1B3 is relative (price), $B$1 is fixed (tax rate)
C4=B4*$B$1B4 updated to row 4; $B$1 stayed fixed ✓
C5=B5*$B$1B5 updated to row 5; $B$1 stayed fixed ✓

Tip: in Excel, press F4 while your cursor is inside a cell reference to cycle through $A$1, A$1, $A1, and A1 automatically.
steps.js: createMCQSet('l7-abs',[{q:'What does $B$1 mean in a formula?',options:['Only row 1 is fixed; column B can change','Only column B is fixed; row 1 can change','Both column B and row 1 are fixed','The formula references a different worksheet'],answer:2,right:'Correct. A $ before the letter locks the column; a $ before the number locks the row. Both here means neither ever changes.'},{q:'A formula must always reference cell D1, no matter where it is copied. Which reference is correct?',options:['$D$1','D1','D$1','$D1'],answer:0,right:'Correct. $D$1 locks both column D and row 1, so the reference cannot shift in any direction.'},{q:'A formula =B3*D$1 is copied one row down. What does the copied formula become?',options:['=B4*D$2','=B4*D$1','=B3*D$1','=B3*D1'],answer:1,right:'Correct. B3 is relative so becomes B4. D$1 has the row fixed at 1 so the row stays as $1.'},{q:'Which reference keeps column B fixed but allows the row number to change when copied down?',options:['B$4','$B$4','B4','$B4'],answer:3,right:'Correct. $B4 locks column B with a $ but the row number 4 has no $ so it will change when copied.'},{q:'How can you quickly cycle through absolute reference options while editing a formula in Excel?',options:['Press Ctrl+D','Press F2','Press Tab','Press F4'],answer:3,right:'Correct. F4 cycles through $A$1, A$1, $A1, and A1 while the cursor is inside a reference.'}]);

steps.title: Practice: Apply Absolute Referencing
steps.content:
Practice: apply absolute referencing

The tax rate (20%) is stored once in cell B1. Write formulas in column C that calculate the tax on each item. Every formula must reference B1 absolutely using $B$1.

CellTask
C4Tax on Pen — multiply the price in B4 by the tax rate in $B$1
C5Tax on Notebook — multiply the price in B5 by the tax rate in $B$1
C6Tax on Folder — multiply the price in B6 by the tax rate in $B$1

Pattern: =B4*$B$1   Each row's price reference changes; the absolute $B$1 stays fixed.
steps.js: (function(){function lcss(u){if(!document.querySelector('link[href="'+u+'"]')){var l=document.createElement('link');l.rel='stylesheet';l.href=u;document.head.appendChild(l);}}function ljs(u,cb){var ex=document.querySelector('script[data-jhncc-src="'+u+'"]');if(ex){if(ex.getAttribute('data-loaded')==='1'){cb();}else{ex.addEventListener('load',function(){cb();});}return;}var s=document.createElement('script');s.setAttribute('data-jhncc-src',u);s.src=u;s.onload=function(){s.setAttribute('data-loaded','1');cb();};s.onerror=function(){alert('The spreadsheet tool could not load. Please refresh the page.');};document.head.appendChild(s);}lcss('assets/css/jsuites.css');lcss('assets/css/jspreadsheet.css');ljs('assets/js/jsuites.js',function(){ljs('assets/js/jspreadsheet.js',function(){ljs('assets/js/jspreadsheet-formula-bar.js?v=22',function(){var data=[['Tax rate (20%)',0.2,''],['','',''],['Item','Price (£)','Tax (£)'],['Pen',2.50,''],['Notebook',5.00,''],['Folder',3.75,'']];var el=document.getElementById('l7-abs-sheet');var sheet=jspreadsheet(el,{data:data,columns:[{width:140},{width:110},{width:120}],minDimensions:[3,6],tableOverflow:true,tableWidth:'100%',toolbar:false,about:false});JHNCCAddFormulaBar(el,sheet);JHNCCAddFormatToolbar(el,sheet);JHNCCAddSheetTabs(el,sheet);document.getElementById('l7-abs-check').onclick=function(){var fb=document.getElementById('l7-abs-fb');var rows=[{row:3,label:'C4',exp:0.50},{row:4,label:'C5',exp:1.00},{row:5,label:'C6',exp:0.75}];var errors=[];rows.forEach(function(c){var raw=String(sheet.getValueFromCoords(2,c.row,false)||'');var val=parseFloat(sheet.getValueFromCoords(2,c.row,true));if(raw.charAt(0)!=='='){errors.push(c.label+' needs a formula starting with =');return;}if(raw.toUpperCase().indexOf('$B$1')===-1){errors.push(c.label+': use $B$1 to reference the tax rate absolutely');}else if(Math.abs(val-c.exp)>0.01){errors.push(c.label+' should equal '+c.exp.toFixed(2)+' (price x 0.2)');}});if(errors.length){fb.className='ex-feedback err';fb.textContent=errors[0];return;}fb.className='ex-feedback ok';fb.textContent='Correct. Each formula uses $B$1 so the tax rate never changes when copied. The price reference (B4, B5, B6) updates automatically for each row.';__markStepComplete();};});});});})();

steps.title: Multiple Worksheets
steps.content:
Multiple worksheets

A spreadsheet workbook is the whole file. Inside the workbook you can have several worksheets (the tabs at the bottom). Each worksheet stores its own data.

TermMeaningExample
WorkbookThe file itselfSalesData.xlsx
WorksheetA tab within the fileSheet1, RawData, Dashboard

You can reference a cell in a different worksheet using the sheet name followed by an exclamation mark, then the cell reference:

Syntax: SheetName!CellRef

FormulaWhat it does
=Constants!B1Reads cell B1 from the sheet called Constants
=RawData!C5Reads cell C5 from the sheet called RawData
=2*$B$1*Constants!A4Multiplies a local $B$1 by A4 from Constants

A common pattern is to store constants (like PI, VAT rate, or a price list) in a sheet called Constants, then reference them from your calculation sheet. If the constant ever needs updating, you change it in one place and all formulas update automatically.
steps.js: createMCQSet('l7-sheets',[{q:'What is the difference between a workbook and a worksheet?',options:['They are the same thing, just different names','A worksheet is the whole .xlsx file; a workbook is one of its tabs','A workbook is the whole .xlsx file; worksheets are the individual tabs inside it','A workbook stores only formulas; a worksheet stores only data'],answer:2,right:'Correct. The workbook is the .xlsx file itself. Each named tab you click at the bottom is a worksheet.'},{q:'PI is stored in cell B1 of a sheet called Constants. Which formula reads it from another sheet?',options:['=Constants.B1','=Constants!B1','=B1(Constants)','=B1!Constants'],answer:1,right:'Correct. SheetName followed by ! then the cell reference is the correct syntax.'},{q:'Why store a constant like a VAT rate on a separate Constants worksheet?',options:['It prevents the value from ever being changed','It automatically formats all cells as currency','Spreadsheets cannot store constants on the main sheet','All formulas reference one place, so one change updates everything'],answer:3,right:'Correct. Centralising constants means you only update a value in one cell and every formula that uses it recalculates.'},{q:'Cell A4 needs an absolute column reference but a relative row. How should it be written?',options:['A$4','$A$4','A4','$A4'],answer:3,right:'Correct. $A4 locks the column A but the row number 4 is relative and will change when the formula is copied down.'}]);

steps.title: Full Practice: Circle Measurements
steps.content:
Full practice: circle measurements

The value of PI is stored once in cell B1. Use it with an absolute reference to calculate the circumference of each circle in column C.

Circumference formula: 2 × PI × radius

CellTask
C4Circumference of Circle A — 2 × $B$1 × B4
C5Circumference of Circle B — 2 × $B$1 × B5
C6Circumference of Circle C — 2 × $B$1 × B6

Pattern: =2*$B$1*B4   PI is locked; the radius reference updates for each row.
steps.js: (function(){function lcss(u){if(!document.querySelector('link[href="'+u+'"]')){var l=document.createElement('link');l.rel='stylesheet';l.href=u;document.head.appendChild(l);}}function ljs(u,cb){var ex=document.querySelector('script[data-jhncc-src="'+u+'"]');if(ex){if(ex.getAttribute('data-loaded')==='1'){cb();}else{ex.addEventListener('load',function(){cb();});}return;}var s=document.createElement('script');s.setAttribute('data-jhncc-src',u);s.src=u;s.onload=function(){s.setAttribute('data-loaded','1');cb();};s.onerror=function(){alert('The spreadsheet tool could not load. Please refresh the page.');};document.head.appendChild(s);}lcss('assets/css/jsuites.css');lcss('assets/css/jspreadsheet.css');ljs('assets/js/jsuites.js',function(){ljs('assets/js/jspreadsheet.js',function(){ljs('assets/js/jspreadsheet-formula-bar.js?v=22',function(){var data=[['PI',3.14159,''],['','',''],['Shape','Radius (cm)','Circumference (cm)'],['Circle A',5,''],['Circle B',8,''],['Circle C',10,'']];var el=document.getElementById('l7-full-sheet');var sheet=jspreadsheet(el,{data:data,columns:[{width:110},{width:130},{width:170}],minDimensions:[3,6],tableOverflow:true,tableWidth:'100%',toolbar:false,about:false});JHNCCAddFormulaBar(el,sheet);JHNCCAddFormatToolbar(el,sheet);JHNCCAddSheetTabs(el,sheet);document.getElementById('l7-full-check').onclick=function(){var fb=document.getElementById('l7-full-fb');var rows=[{row:3,label:'C4',rRef:'B4',exp:31.416},{row:4,label:'C5',rRef:'B5',exp:50.265},{row:5,label:'C6',rRef:'B6',exp:62.832}];var errors=[];rows.forEach(function(c){var raw=String(sheet.getValueFromCoords(2,c.row,false)||'');var rawUp=raw.toUpperCase();var val=parseFloat(sheet.getValueFromCoords(2,c.row,true));if(raw.charAt(0)!=='='){errors.push(c.label+' needs a formula starting with =');return;}if(rawUp.indexOf('$B$1')===-1){errors.push(c.label+': reference PI using $B$1 (absolute reference)');}else if(rawUp.indexOf(c.rRef.toUpperCase())===-1){errors.push(c.label+': include the radius cell '+c.rRef+' in your formula');}else if(Math.abs(val-c.exp)>0.2){errors.push(c.label+' should be approximately '+c.exp.toFixed(2)+' cm');}});if(errors.length){fb.className='ex-feedback err';fb.textContent=errors[0];return;}fb.className='ex-feedback ok';fb.textContent='Correct. PI is locked as $B$1 in every formula. If PI were updated in B1, all three circumferences would recalculate automatically.';__markStepComplete();};});});});})();

steps.title: Check What You Have Learned
steps.content:
Check what you have learned

These questions cover relative and absolute cell referencing and multiple worksheets.
steps.js: createMCQSet('l7-exam',[{q:'A formula =C5*D5 is copied one row down. What is in the new cell?',options:['=C5*D5','=C6*D5','=C6*D6','=C5*D6'],answer:2,right:'Correct. Both references are relative so both row numbers increase from 5 to 6.'},{q:'A discount rate is in cell C1. Which reference ensures C1 is always used even when the formula is copied down ten rows?',options:['C1','C$1','$C1','$C$1'],answer:3,right:'Correct. $C$1 locks both column C and row 1, so neither part of the reference can shift.'},{q:'A formula =$D$2*B5 is copied one row down. What does the copied formula contain?',options:['=$D$3*B6','=$D$2*B6','=$D$3*B5','=$D$2*B5'],answer:1,right:'Correct. $D$2 is fully absolute so both parts stay fixed. B5 is relative so the row increases to B6.'},{q:'What is the difference between a workbook and a worksheet?',options:['A workbook is a single tab; a worksheet is the whole file','They mean the same thing in modern Excel','A workbook is the whole file; worksheets are the named tabs inside it','A worksheet contains formulas only; a workbook contains data only'],answer:2,right:'Correct. The workbook is the .xlsx file; each named tab at the bottom of the screen is a worksheet.'},{q:'A sheet called Constants holds the VAT rate in cell B2. Which formula reads it from another sheet?',options:['=B2(Constants)','=Constants.B2','=B2!Constants','=Constants!B2'],answer:3,right:'Correct. The correct syntax is SheetName followed by an exclamation mark then the cell reference.'},{q:'Which reference allows the row to change when copied down, but keeps the column fixed at B?',options:['$B$5','B5','B$5','$B5'],answer:3,right:'Correct. $B5 locks column B with a $. The row number 5 has no $, so it updates when copied down.'}]);

steps.title: Endless Practice
steps.content:
Endless practice

Answer 5 in a row correctly to finish.
steps.js:
(function(){var bank=[{q:'Which symbol locks a cell reference in a formula?',o:['# (hash)','$ (dollar sign)','@ (at sign)','& (ampersand)'],a:1},{q:'What does $C$3 mean in a formula?',o:['Both column C and row 3 are fixed','Only row 3 is fixed; the column can change','Only column C is fixed; the row can change','Neither is fixed; it is the same as C3'],a:0},{q:'A formula =B4*$C$1 is copied one row down. What is the new formula?',o:['=B5*$C$1','=B4*$C$1','=B5*$C$2','=B4*$C$2'],a:0},{q:'When should you use an absolute reference?',o:['When every row should use its own values from the same columns','When you want the formula to produce an error','When a cell holds a constant that every row in the formula must share','When copying will always produce the right result anyway'],a:2},{q:'What is a workbook?',o:['A single tab within a spreadsheet file','The formula bar at the top of the screen','A type of chart used in dashboards','The entire spreadsheet file, such as SalesData.xlsx'],a:3},{q:'Which syntax reads cell A1 from a sheet called Data?',o:['=Data.A1','=A1/Data','=A1!Data','=Data!A1'],a:3},{q:'A tax rate in B1 must not change when the formula is copied down column C. What reference is used?',o:['B1','$B1','B$1','$B$1'],a:3},{q:'A formula =A$5*B3 is copied one column to the right. What is the new formula?',o:['=A$5*C3','=B$5*C3','=B$5*B3','=A$5*B3'],a:1},{q:'Cell A4 must have its column fixed but its row remain relative. Which is correct?',o:['A$4','$A$4','A4','$A4'],a:3},{q:'Why store a constant in a separate Constants worksheet?',o:['It prevents any formula from reading it accidentally','It makes the file smaller','Changing it in one cell updates all formulas that reference it','It formats the cell as currency automatically'],a:2}];var solved=0,c=document.getElementById('l7-endless');function pick(){return bank[Math.floor(Math.random()*bank.length)];}function render(){var q=pick();var opts=q.o.map(function(x,i){return{x:x,i:i};}).sort(function(){return Math.random()-0.5;});c.innerHTML='

Correct in a row: '+solved+' / 5

'+q.q+'

'+opts.map(function(o){return '';}).join('')+'
';c.querySelectorAll('button').forEach(function(btn){btn.onclick=function(){var fb=c.querySelector('.ex-feedback');if(parseInt(btn.dataset.i)===q.a){solved++;fb.className='ex-feedback ok';fb.textContent=solved>=5?'Practice complete.':'Correct. Next question.';if(solved>=5)__markStepComplete();else setTimeout(render,700);}else{solved=0;fb.className='ex-feedback err';fb.textContent='Not quite — streak reset.';setTimeout(render,1000);}};});}render();})();

quizQuestions.q: A formula in E4 is =C4*D4. It is copied to E5. What does E5 contain?
A VAT rate is stored in B1 and must not move when the formula is copied down. Which reference is correct?

The tax rate (20%) is in B1. In C4, write a formula to calculate the tax on the Pen using an absolute reference to B1. In C5, do the same for the Notebook. Do not type the tax rate as a number — reference B1 absolutely.
A formula =$E$1*B3 is copied one row down. What does the copied formula become?
A sheet called Rates holds a currency exchange rate in cell C3. Which formula reads it from a different sheet?

options: The entire spreadsheet file saved on disk
A named tab within a workbook
A formula that spans multiple cells
A type of chart used in dashboards

quizQuestions.q: PI (3.14159) is in B1. In C4 and C5, write formulas for the circumference of each circle (2 × PI × radius). Reference PI absolutely — do not type 3.14159 directly.
Which reference locks only the row at row 2, leaving the column relative?
A constant is stored in a separate worksheet and referenced absolutely. What is the benefit if the constant needs to change?
options: Every formula that uses it must be updated individually
The formula automatically deletes the old value
Changing the one cell updates every formula that references it
The spreadsheet creates a backup copy before changing
